title: NYSE Arca Equities - CEE Ruling in UVIX - 2X Long VIX Futures ETF
raw_text:
NYSE Arca Equities - CEE Ruling in UVIX - 2X Long VIX Futures ETF
Markets: NYSE ARCA EQUITIES; Services: Trading
NYSE Arca in conjunction with other UTP exchanges, has ruled to bust all erroneous trades in UVIX - 2X Long VIX Futures ETF, executed on NYSE Arca between 04:00:00 and 04:30:00 ET today, at or below $58.10. This ruling is not eligible for appeal.

venue: NYSE American Options / NYSE Arca Options
title: NYSE Options - July 2026 Fee Changes
raw_text:
NYSE Options - July 2026 Fee Changes
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Effective July 1, 2026, NYSE Options will adopt the following fee changes: Introduce an MM/LMM Posting Incentive Program for Designated Non-Penny Issues on NYSE Arca Options, pending effectiveness of a regulatory filing. Implement a new methodology for determining Options Regulatory Fees ("ORF") and modify rates to $0.0160 on NYSE American Options and $0.0120 on NYSE Arca Options, as previously announced . Market Maker Posting Incentive Program for Designated Non-Penny Issues Under the new program, Market Makers are eligible to receive an additional $0.40 per contract credit on top of the base $0.05 MM and $0.40 LMM Non-Penny Post Liquidity credits, when the combined rate is higher than the applicable credit under the existing Non-Customer, Non-Penny Posting Credit Tiers. This program will apply to new listings that trade more than 1 million contracts in industry volume, as reported by OCC, on their first day of listing, and will remain in effect until the symbol is added to the Penny Interval Program. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this update.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities for Q3 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities for Q3 2026
Listing Alert MIAX Pearl July 01, 2026 | 02:32 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities for Q3 2026 As previously announced in the January 20, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options Exchanges filed with the SEC to amend the Short Term Option Series Program of each exchange to permit the listing of up to two Monday and Wednesday expirations for options on certain individual stocks or Exchange-Traded Fund ("ETF") Shares (“Qualifying Securities”). A Qualifying Security is an individual stock or ETF that, on a quarterly basis: (1) has an underlying security, as measured on the last day of the prior calendar quarter, with: (A) a market capitalization of greater than 700 billion dollars for an individual stock based on the closing price, or (B) Assets under Management (“AUM”) greater than 50 billion dollars for an Exchange-Traded Fund Share based on net asset value (“NAV”); (2) has monthly options volume, as measured by sides traded in the last month preceding the quarter end, of greater than 10 million options; (3) has a position limit of at least 250,000 contracts; and (4) participates in the Penny Interval Program. A Qualifying Security expiration would not be listed on a day when there will be an earnings announcement that takes place after market close. The following securities are Qualified Securities for Q3 2026: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Alphabet, Inc. (GOOGL) Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Apple Inc. (AAPL) Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) Intel Corporation (INTC) Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Micron Technology, Inc. (MU) Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) Each calendar quarter, the above criteria will be reassessed to determine eligibility for the following quarter as a Qualifying Security. The MIAX Option Exchanges will post the list of Qualifying Securities on our website by the close of business on the first trading day of each quarter. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308.

raw_text:

Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Effective July 2, 2026, NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options will update the list of Qualifying Securities permitted to list Monday and Wednesday expirations under the Short Term Options Series Program. Eligibility Requirements To qualify, a security must meet the following criteria on a quarterly basis: Individual stocks with a market capitalization exceeding $700 billion (based on closing price), or ETFs with Assets Under Management exceeding $50 billion (based on net asset value) Monthly options volume exceeding 10 million contracts in the final month of the preceding quarter A position limit of at least 250,000 contracts Inclusion in the Penny Interval Program Securities that no longer meet the above criteria will be removed from the program on the same date that the updated list becomes effective. title: NYSE American - New Security Notification for Symbol COPR WS

NYSE American - New Security Notification for Symbol COPR WS
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES; Services: Trading
NYSE American has determined that the Common Stock Purchase Warrant issued by Idaho Copper Corporation is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker COPR WS with CUSIP 481159119 on July 2, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security COPR WS until its second day of trading on NYSE American. Further, NYSE American Rule 7.18E(e) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE American has opened trading.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: American Bitcoin Corporation (ABTC)
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: American Bitcoin Corporation (ABTC)
Listing Alert MIAX Sapphire July 02, 2026 | 12:35 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: American Bitcoin Corporation (ABTC) American Bitcoin Corporation (ABTC) has announced a reverse stock split effective for Monday, July 6, 2026. ABTC1 will be available for trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Monday, July 6, 2026. ABTC1 will not be available for trading on the MIAX Emerald Options Exchange. No options on the underlying ABTC will be available for trading on Monday, July 6, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in ABTC will be canceled at the close of business on Thursday, July 2, 2026. ABTC will resume trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Tuesday July 7, 2026. Please Note: ABTC1 options will trade on Cloud 1 on the MIAX Options Exchange, the MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange. Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. ABTC should be used for class assignments in ABTC1 via MFP All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. All changes made after 6:00 PM ET on a given day will be effective two trading days later. MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Primary Lead Market Maker (PLMM) assignments and un-assignments will not be supported via the MFP. title: Notice Regarding IEX Exchange Fee Schedule Updates Effective October 1, 2026 for TOPS, DEEP and DEEP+
raw_text:
Notice Regarding IEX Exchange Fee Schedule Updates Effective October 1, 2026 for TOPS, DEEP and DEEP+
Category: Market Data and Connectivity; Alert 2026-008
Overview IEX Exchange is issuing this Trading Alert to notify Members and all market data recipients of upcoming changes to the IEX Exchange Market Data Fee Schedule. Effective October 1, 2026, IEX Exchange will: (1) increase the monthly fee for the Real-Time TOPS Feed from $500 to $1,000 per month; and (2) introduce Non-Display fees for the Real-Time DEEP Feed ($2,500 per month) and the Real-Time DEEP+ Feed ($3,500 per month), subject to the filing and effectiveness of an SEC rule filing. Fee Change 1: TOPS Feed - Price Increase TOPS (Top of Book Quote and Last Sale) is IEX Exchange’s Real-Time top-of-book market data feed. TOPS provides real-time top of book quotations and last sale information for executions on IEX Exchange, showing aggregated size at the best bid and ask. Effective October 1, the monthly fee for the Real-Time TOPS Feed will increase from $500 per month to $1,000 per month. The following table summarizes the change: Service Current Fee New Fee (Effective Oct. 1) TOPS Feed (Real-Time) $500 per month $1,000 per month Fee Change 2: Introduction of Non-Display Fees for DEEP and DEEP+ Feeds DEEP (Depth of Book and Last Sale) is IEX Exchange’s Real-Time depth-of-book market data feed, providing aggregated size of resting displayed orders at each price level. DEEP+ (Order-by-Order Depth of Book and Last Sale) provides an order-by-order view of resting displayed orders on IEX Exchange. Both feeds also provide last trade price and size information for executions on IEX Exchange. As defined in IEX Market Data Policies , “Non-Display” use refers to accessing, processing, or consuming Real-Time IEX Market Data for a purpose other than (i) solely facilitating Data Subscriber’s display of the Real-Time IEX Market Data or (ii) solely internally or externally distributing the Real-Time IEX Market Data. Non-Display uses include, without limitation, use in automated order generation or routing, algorithmic or program trading, risk management, surveillance, and any other machine-driven use of market data. Effective October 1, IEX Exchange will introduce Non-Display fees for recipients of the Real-Time DEEP and Real-Time DEEP+ feeds. Recipients who access or use either feed for Non-Display purposes will be subject to the applicable Non-Display fee in addition to any other applicable IEX Market Data fees. The following table summarizes the new Non-Display fees: Service Real-Time Access Fee (per month) Non-Display Fee (per month, NEW) DEEP Feed (Real-Time) $2,500 $2,500 DEEP+ Feed (Real-Time) $3,500 $3,500 Updated Market Data Fee Schedule The updates that will be effective October 1 are illustrated below. Service Real-Time Access Fee Non-Display Fee TOPS Feed (Real-Time) $1,000 per month — DEEP Feed (Real-Time) $2,500 per month $2,500 per month DEEP+ Feed (Real-Time) $3,500 per month $3,500 per month All IEX Market Data delayed 15 minutes or more is free and available via IEX’s robust network of Third Party providers . Additional Resources IEX Rule Book IEX Fee Schedule IEX Data Subscriber Agreement IEX Market Data Policies IEX Market Data Specifications Contact Information Please contact the IEX Market Data Team or Market Operations with any questions: Market Data: iexmarketdata@iextrading.com Market Operations: marketops@iextrading.com About The Investors Exchange Investors’ Exchange LLC (IEX Exchange) is a national securities exchange designed to provide superior execution quality and set a new standard for trading. Since launching in 2016, the exchange has grown to be one of the largest exchange operators globally, by notional value traded. In 2026, IEX plans to launch IEX Options, a new U.S. options exchange that will bring its suite of order protection innovations designed to better address the needs of market makers and drive better outcomes for end investors. To learn more visit iex.io .

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: The Middleby Corporation (MIDD) and Service Properties Trust (SVC)
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: The Middleby Corporation (MIDD) and Service Properties Trust (SVC)
Listing Alert MIAX Pearl July 06, 2026 | 11:01 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: The Middleby Corporation (MIDD) and Service Properties Trust (SVC) The Middleby Corporation (MIDD) has announced a distribution and Service Properties Trust (SVC) has announced a reverse stock split effective for Tuesday, July 7, 2026. The MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges will not list MIDD1 and SVC1 options. Therefore, no options on MIDD and SVC will be available for trading on Tuesday, July 7, 2026. The Middleby Corporation (MIDD) and Service Properties Trust (SVC) will resume trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Wednesday, July 8, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in MIDD and SVC will be canceled at the close of business on Monday, July 6, 2026. Additional details about the above corporate actions are provided in the attached OCC Memos. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308. MIDD Distribution 59284 SVC Reverse Split 59295

title: MIAX Pearl Equities - Amendments to MIAX Pearl Equities Rules to Establish Common Criteria and Procedures for Halting and Resuming Trading in Equity Securities in the Event of Regulatory or Operational Issues
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities - Amendments to MIAX Pearl Equities Rules to Establish Common Criteria and Procedures for Halting and Resuming Trading in Equity Securities in the Event of Regulatory or Operational Issues
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Pearl Equities July 06, 2026 | 10:48 AM MIAX Pearl Equities - Amendments to MIAX Pearl Equities Rules to Establish Common Criteria and Procedures for Halting and Resuming Trading in Equity Securities in the Event of Regulatory or Operational Issues Effective August 10, 2026, MIAX Pearl Exchange, Inc., in conjunction with adoption of the Amended CTA/CQ Plan proposed by its participants, will adopt Rules 2627 and 2628 to integrate several definitions and concepts from the Amended CTA/CQ Plan, reorganize existing Rule 2622 in light of the Exchange’s experience with applying the rule during its time as a national securities exchange, and make conforming changes to related rules. For more information on the amendments to the Rules, please refer to the following Regulatory Circular: MIAX Pearl Equities RC 2026-06 Contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 with any questions regarding the amendments. Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (EOSE1)
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (EOSE1)
Listing Alert MIAX Sapphire July 06, 2026 | 03:01 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (EOSE1) Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (EOSE1) will be available for trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Tuesday, July 7, 2026. EOSE1 will not be available for trading on the MIAX Emerald Options Exchange. Please Note: EOSE1 options will trade on Cloud 5 on the MIAX Options Exchange, on Cloud 3 on the MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and on Cloud 2 on the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange. Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. EOSE should be used for class assignments in EOSE1 via MFP All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. All changes made after 6:00 PM ET on a given day will be effective two trading days later. MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Primary Lead Market Maker (PLMM) assignments and un-assignments will not be supported via the MFP. Additional details about the above Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (EOSE) distribution are provided in the attached OCC Memo. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308. EOSE Distribution 59297 0

DTN2026-11 - SK hynix Inc. Initial Public Offering on Nasdaq Global Select Market
raw_text:
DTN2026-11 - SK hynix Inc. Initial Public Offering on Nasdaq Global Select Market
News CategoryAlertMarketsWhat You Need To Know
Data Technical News#2026-011The Nasdaq Stock Market

SK hynix Inc. is expected to be listed and begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on July 10, 2026, on a when issued basis with the symbol SKHYV.

The symbol is expected to change to SKHY and begin trading on a regular way basis effective July 13, 2026.

The settlement date for all when-issued trades is expected to be July 14, 2026.

Nasdaq requests that market data distributors update their databases to reflect this upcoming listing and display the symbol at their earliest opportunity.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: T-REX 2X Long BMNR Daily Target ETF (BMNU)
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: T-REX 2X Long BMNR Daily Target ETF (BMNU)
Listing Alert MIAX Sapphire July 10, 2026 | 10:11 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: T-REX 2X Long BMNR Daily Target ETF (BMNU) T-REX 2X Long BMNR Daily Target ETF (BMNU) has announced a reverse stock split effective for Monday, July 13, 2026. BMNU1 will be available for trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Monday, July 13, 2026. BMNU1 will not be available for trading on the MIAX Emerald Options Exchange. No options on the underlying BMNU will be available for trading on Monday, July 13, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in BMNU will be canceled at the close of business on Friday, July 10, 2026. BMNU will resume trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Tuesday July 14, 2026. Please Note: BMNU1 options will trade on Cloud 2 on the MIAX Options Exchange, the MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and Cloud 2 on the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange. Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. BMNU should be used for class assignments in BMNU1 via MFP All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. All changes made after 6:00 PM ET on a given day will be effective two trading days later. MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Primary Lead Market Maker (PLMM) assignments and un-assignments will not be supported via the MFP. T-REX 2X Long BMNR Daily Target ETF (BMNU) reverse split are provided in the attached OCC Memo. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308. BMNU Reverse Split 59337

title: FINRA/NYSE TRF® - Extended Operating Hours Effective December 6, 2026
raw_text:
FINRA/NYSE TRF® - Extended Operating Hours Effective December 6, 2026
Markets: FINRA/NYSE TRF; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Effective December 6, 2026, FINRA/NYSE TRF® ("NYSE TRF") will extend its operating hours in alignment with the recently approved CTA and UTP Plan amendments to extend the operating hours of the Securities Information Processors, pursuant to the proposed rule change to amend the FINRA Rule 6300 Series . NYSE TRF will operate from 9:00 p.m. ET Sunday through 8:00 p.m. ET Friday, with a one-hour pause between 8:00 p.m. and 9:00 p.m. ET Monday through Thursday, excluding holidays. This expands on the current operating hours of 4:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, excluding holidays. The NYSE TRF Pillar FIX system specifications have been updated to reflect the upcoming extended operating hours. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this change.

title: IEX Exchange Updates to Trading Halt Rules
raw_text:
IEX Exchange Updates to Trading Halt Rules
Category: Regulatory; Alert 2026-009
Overview Effective August 10, 2026, IEX Exchange will make operative certain previously approved amendments to its respective trading halt rules that are the result of coordinated work by IEX Exchange and the other SROs to establish common criteria and procedures for halting and resuming trading in equity securities when regulatory or operational issues arise. The amendments incorporate several definitions and concepts from the amended Nasdaq UTP Plan and the Amended CTA/CQ Plans (SIP Plans) and are intended to promote consistent and transparent treatment of events that may affect multiple exchanges. IEX believes that these common standards will help the SROs maintain fair and orderly markets. IEX will continue to retain discretion in certain instances as to whether and how to handle halts. Once operative as scheduled, the updated rules: Clarify the situations that form the basis of a Primary Listing Market’s authority to declare a Regulatory Halt: (1) as provided by the SIP Plans; (2) discretionary Regulatory Halts; and (3) mandatory Regulatory Halts; Provide detailed and consistent rules on how a Primary Listing Market will initiate and resume trading in the event of a Regulatory Halt and clarifies the obligations of other exchanges under the circumstances; and Clarify the discretion afforded to IEX to impose an Operational Halt that applies only to the IEX equities market. Testing Opportunity Saturday User Acceptance Testing (UAT) production testing will be available on August 8. More Information Rule Filing: SR-IEX-2025-37 Nasdaq UTP Plan Amendment 50 CTA Plan Amendment 36/CQ Plan Amendment 27 Contact Information Please contact IEX Market Operations with any questions: Market Operations: marketops@iextrading.com About The Investors Exchange Investors’ Exchange LLC (IEX Exchange) is a national securities exchange designed to provide superior execution quality and set a new standard for trading. Since launching in 2016, the exchange has grown to be one of the largest exchange operators globally, by notional value traded. In 2026, IEX plans to launch IEX Options, a new U.S. options exchange that will bring its suite of order protection innovations designed to better address the needs of market makers and drive better outcomes for end investors. To learn more visit iex.io .

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Reminder: Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Reminder: Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance
Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Options July 13, 2026 | 10:27 AM MIAX Options Exchange - Reminder: Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance As previously announced in the June 18, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options Exchange will rebalance symbol to cloud allocations effective Monday, July 20, 2026. Application layer connectivity testing will be available for firms to confirm changes on Saturday, July 18, 2026 between 8:30 a.m. and 10:30 a.m. ET. Please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com to arrange testing . Symbol Range to Cloud Allocation: A (with the exception of symbols AAPL, AMD and AMZN) B C D-F G-H (with the exception of symbols GOOG and GOOGL) I J-L M (with the exception of symbols META and MU) N O P-R (with the exception of symbol QQQ) S (with the exception of symbols SNDK, SPCX and SPY) T-U (with the exception of symbol TSLA) V-Y Z AAPL, AMZN AMD GOOG, GOOGL META, SNDK MU QQQ TSLA SPCX SPY Market Data Dissemination: Market Data Feed content for each symbol will be disseminated across the assigned Cloud multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed MIAX Express Interface (MEI) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options to their MEI sessions for each Cloud FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted For additional details, please visit MIAX Options Interface Specifications . If you have any questions or to arrange testing, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302.

title: NYSE Group: Market Wide Circuit Breaker Test July 25, 2026
raw_text:
NYSE Group: Market Wide Circuit Breaker Test July 25, 2026
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
On Saturday, July 25, 2026, NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE Arca, NYSE National and NYSE Texas will participate in the Securities Information Processors' (SIPs) industry testing of Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Level 1 and 2. NYSE Group equities markets will be available from their primary data sites in Mahwah from 9:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. ET. Designated required participants for the October 2026 Reg. SCI test on NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE Arca, NYSE National and NYSE Texas are required to participate in at least one MWCB test, per year. Firms required to test have been notified of their requirement. The script for the test can be found here . The testing requirements below must be completed for each NYSE Group equities exchange for which the firm has been designated as a required tester: Receive and process MWCB halt messages from the SIPs. Receive and process resume messages from the SIPs following a MWCB halt. Receive and process market data from the SIPs relevant to the MWCB halt. Send orders following a Level 1 or Level 2 MWCB halt in a manner consistent with usual trading behavior. Firms should register to test at trading@nyse.com to have their participation recorded. Following the test, firms must return the participation checklist to Regscitesting@nyse.com . Remaining 2026 SIP Failover/MWCB Saturday Testing Dates July 25, 2026 - SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #2 September 12, 2026 - MWCB Level 1/2/3 Test December 5, 2026 - SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #3 Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this update.

title: Testing Opportunity Reminder: OPRA-GTH System Hardware Upgrade Industry Test #1 - Saturday, July 18, 2026
raw_text:
Testing Opportunity Reminder: OPRA-GTH System Hardware Upgrade Industry Test #1 - Saturday, July 18, 2026
July 10, 2026 TO: OPRA Multicast Data Subscribers SUBJECT: Testing Opportunity Reminder: OPRA-GTH System Hardware Upgrade Industry Test #1 - Saturday, July 18, 2026 What You Should Know As previously announced, SIAC will be upgrading the OPRA-GTH (Global Trading Hours) Pillar platform to next generation hardware. The activation date for this upgrade will be Sunday, August 23, 2026. What is Changing? • Although this upgrade does not impact the multicast feed, the OPRA SIP is offering functional testing for this change. Testing Opportunities and Activation • Industry Test #1: Saturday, July 18, 2026 • Industry Test #2: Saturday, August 8, 2026 • Confidence Test: Saturday, August 22, 2026 • Activation: Sunday, August 23, 2026 The test plan and schedule can be found on the next page of this notice. Test Registration • Each OPRA Data Subscriber participating in the test should register at CTA-OPRA- Support@siac.com. Data Subscribers who receive OPRA from connectivity service providers other than ICE Global Network (IGN) or the NMS Network must contact their connectivity service providers to coordinate testing. Technical Inquiries • NMS Product Management Support Email: CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com • NMS Product Management Support Line: 212.656.8177, Option 2 (Monday through Friday, 9:00AM - 5:00PM ET) Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 yy 2 Test Setup - Primary Data Center: New Hardware (no changes to IP/Multicast) Backup Data Center: Current Hardware # Time Test Category Action By Test Description Expected Results Friday, July 17 1 7:45:00 PM - 8:30:00 PM SOD OPRA OPRA Global Trading Hours (GTH) to trigger Start of Day message CBOE to establish connectivity for OPRA GTH Input lines on Primary Data Center CBOE establish connections CBOE to start submitting Quote and Trade data Quotes and trades accepted and disseminated via the multicast output lines in Primary Data Center Saturday, July 18 3 7:30:00 AM - 7:45:00 AM Data Verification CBOE CBOE to continue submitting Quote and Trade data Data Subscribers can verify Data over the Output Multicast lines 4 7:45:00 AM - 8:00:00 AM Output Publication Primary Process Failure OPRA OPRA GTH to simulate Primary Output Publication process failure. Data Publication to switch to backup process -- Subscribers may see a momentary outage in data flow on the output lines -- Subscriber can request retransmission for any gaps OPRA OPRA GTH to simulate primary input line failure of a subset of OPRA input lines for CBOE CBOE will see primary input lines disconnected CBOE For the affected lines, CBOE to reconnect to OPRA GTH on their backup lines and establish sequence numbers by sending Block Sequence Number Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type L) and Message Count Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type R). CBOE to start submitting Quote and Trade data -- OPRA GTH to accept input connections and respond with Block Sequence Response (Category N / Type M) and Message Count Status Response (Category N / Type S) -- Quotes and trades accepted and disseminated on the output lines OPRA GTH to simulate site failure on Primary Data Center -- Disconnection of all Input and Retransmission lines to OPRA GTH -- Disruption in output multicast data dissemination for all lines OPRA GTH to failover to Backup Data Center and publish following messages: 1) Reset Block Sequence Number (Category H Type K) for each OPRA output line to reset the sequence number to 1 2) Disaster Recovery Data Center Activation (Category H Type P) message 3) Zero Quotes (Quote messages with Zero Price and Size) on behalf of CBOE across all symbols -- Subscribers to receive and process sequence reset message (Category H / Type K) disseminated from the DR site -- Subscribers to receive the Disaster Recovery Site Activation Control Message (Category H, Type P) over the multicast lines -- Subscribers to receive the zero quotes disseminated upon failover Note: To request retransmission for any range of messages disseminated after the sequence reset to 1, Data Subscriber would be required to apply the offset up to 4,294,967,295 in the message sequence number OPRA GTH to enable input lines and Retransmission lines of the DR site DR Input IP/Ports available to establish connections CBOE to reconnect to input IP/ports on OPRA GTH DR site and establish sequence numbers by sending Block Sequence Number Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type L) and Message Count Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type R). OPRA GTH to accept input connections and respond with Block Sequence Response (Category N / Type M) and Message Count Status Response (Category N / Type S) CBOE to start submitting Options data from the latest Sequence Numbers -- Subscribers to accept Quotes and trades disseminated on the output lines. -- Subscribers can request Retransmission for any gaps via the retransmission lines on the DR site. CBOE to start submitting Quote and Trade data -- Subscribers can verify Data over the DR Output Multicast lines 9:15:00 AM End of test OPRA Global Trading Hours (GTH) Hardware Upgrade Test Date & Time: July 18, 2026, 7:30 AM to 9:15 AM ET (Approximate) 5 8:00:00 AM - 8:15:00 AM Participant Line Disconnect 8:15:00 AM - 8:45:00 AM Site failover from Primary Data Center (LDC) to Disaster Recovery Data Center (RDC)6 OPRA CBOE Hourly Test Script 2 7:45:00 PM - 7:30:00 AM Participant Connectivity/Input CBOE

title: NYSE Arca - Extended Hours Trading Effective December 6, 2026
raw_text:
NYSE Arca - Extended Hours Trading Effective December 6, 2026
Markets: NYSE ARCA EQUITIES; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning Sunday, December 6, 2026 , NYSE Arca will operate 23 hours a day. An Overnight Session will begin at 9:00 PM each evening, Sunday through Thursday, and end at 8:00 PM the following day. If a US exchange holiday falls between Monday and Friday, the Exchange will conclude its Late Session trading the evening before the holiday. The next Overnight Session will begin at 9:00 PM the evening of the holiday, if the following day is a trading day. All times are ET. NYSE Arca Extended Trading Hours Functionality New Trading Session : An Overnight Session will be introduced as a fourth distinct session on the existing NYSE Arca platform, covering all NMS securities from 9:00 PM to 4:00 AM. The Overnight Session will open with continuous trading at 9:00 PM with no opening auction; order entry will open at 8:59 PM. The existing Early, Core and Late Sessions will not change. Connectivity : Firms will use their existing NYSE Pillar Gateway ports and submit a new TradingSessionID value to participate in the Overnight Session. Risk Controls : Existing risk limits (e.g., Gross Credit) will be available in the Overnight Session as in all other sessions. In addition, Entering and Clearing firms may opt clients in or out of the Overnight Session at the MPID or MPID + SubID level. Order Protection : Clearly Erroneous Execution (CEE) protections will be extended to the Overnight Session with doubled numerical guidelines. Limit Order Price Protection will apply as in the Early and Late Sessions. Market Data : Consolidated and NYSE Arca proprietary data feeds will be available throughout the extended operating schedule on existing publication channels. A new Security Status message value of 'n' will be added for the Overnight Session in the Security Status and Market State fields. Specifications : Updated system specifications will be distributed via Trader Update in August. Testing In conjunction with the Securities Information Processors , the Exchange will conduct six tests ahead of the December 6 Production launch. Tests will begin at 11:00 PM on Friday and conclude at 12:00 PM on Saturday. The scheduled industry test dates are: October 2 | October 16 | October 30 | November 6 | November 20 | December 4 Beginning November 8, 2026, NYSE Arca will support trading of test symbols in Production during the Overnight Session. Regulatory Updates LULD Plan Amendment : The LULD Plan has filed an amendment with the SEC to create Overnight Price Bands, which would be in effect from 9:00 PM to 4:00 AM, calculated using two reference prices: the primary listing market's official closing price and the consolidated last round lot sale as of 7:45 PM. NYSE Arca is prepared to implement these changes if the SEC approves the LULD Plan amendment. The lower band would be set 20% below the lower reference price; the upper band would be set 20% above the higher reference price. Unlike LULD Bands in the Core Session, limit states would not be calculated by the SIPs and there would be no automatic Trading Pauses. Primary Listing Markets would instead have discretion to declare a Regulatory Halt for the remainder of the Overnight Session. Corporate Actions : The primary listing markets have agreed on harmonized rules for mandatory Regulatory Halts affecting securities subject to certain corporate actions. These rules will provide exchanges and market participants adequate time to process complex corporate actions and make adjustments to systems, orders, quotes, and reference data. For details, see the Corporate Action Halt Rules FAQ . For additional information, visit nyse.com/extended-hours-trading or review the Extended Hours Trading FAQs . Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with questions about these updates.

Bear 2X ETF (DRIP), Fomento Economico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V. (FMX), and Direxion Daily Technology Bear 3X ETF (TECS) have announced corporate actions effective for Wednesday, July 15, 2026. NYSE, NYSE American Equities, NYSE Arca Equities, NYSE National, NYSE Texas, NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options permit holders and member organizations are required to submit any changes to their list of personnel authorized to conduct business over the phone or email with the NYSE Trading Operations Desk. Services affected include oral requests for: Order cancellations and bulk cancellations Firm or line ID execution reports Order status and order history Any other firm-sensitive information Firms may submit updates using the Authorized Trader Submission portal or email to authorized.trader@nyse.com .

title: NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol DGAC and DGAC RT
raw_text:
NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol DGAC and DGAC RT
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
NYSE has determined that the Class A Ordinary Shares issued by Disciplined Growth Acquisition Corporation is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker DGAC with CUSIP G2775W101 on July 17, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security DGAC until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading. NYSE has determined that the Rights, each right entitling the holder to receive one-fourth (1/4) of one Class A ordinary share issued by Disciplined Growth Acquisition Corporation is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker DGAC RT with CUSIP G2775W135 on July 17, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security DGAC RT until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading.

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Final Reminder: Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Final Reminder: Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Options July 17, 2026 | 10:41 AM MIAX Options Exchange - Final Reminder: Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance As previously announced in the June 18, 2026 and July 13, 2026 Alerts, the MIAX Options Exchange will rebalance symbol to cloud allocations effective Monday, July 20, 2026. Application layer connectivity testing will be available for firms to confirm changes on Saturday, July 18, 2026 between 8:30 a.m. and 10:30 a.m. ET. Please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com to arrange testing . Symbol Range to Cloud Allocation: A (with the exception of symbols AAPL, AMD and AMZN) B C D-F G-H (with the exception of symbols GOOG and GOOGL) I J-L M (with the exception of symbols META and MU) N O P-R (with the exception of symbol QQQ) S (with the exception of symbols SNDK, SPCX and SPY) T-U (with the exception of symbol TSLA) V-Y Z AAPL, AMZN AMD GOOG, GOOGL META, SNDK MU QQQ TSLA SPCX SPY Market Data Dissemination: Market Data Feed content for each symbol will be disseminated across the assigned Cloud multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed MIAX Express Interface (MEI) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options to their MEI sessions for each Cloud FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted For additional details, please visit MIAX Options Interface Specifications . If you have any questions or to arrange testing, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302.

title: NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol VII and VII WS
raw_text:
NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol VII and VII WS
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
NYSE has determined that the Class A Ordinary Shares issued by Viking Acquisition Corp. II is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker VII with CUSIP G9601D108 on July 20, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security VII until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading. NYSE has determined that the Redeemable warrants, each full warrant exercisable for one Class A ordinary share at an exercise price of $11.50 per share issued by Viking Acquisition Corp. II is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker VII WS with CUSIP G9601D124 on July 20, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security VII WS until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading.

Product Alert, Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Futures July 17, 2026 | 04:55 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Approved Application for Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat Regularity MIAX Futures has approved the application of Columbia Grain International LLC to be regular for delivery of Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat, effective August 1, 2026, at the following Facilities: Facility Delivery Location Maximum Capacity Arvilla North Dakota/Minnesota Territory 2,390,000 Crystal North Dakota/Minnesota Territory 1,450,000 Jamestown North Dakota/Minnesota Territory 2,345,000 New Rockford North Dakota/Minnesota Territory 1,990,000 Rogers North Dakota/Minnesota Territory 3,385,000 Valley City North Dakota/Minnesota Territory 2,550,000 Total Capacity: 14,110,000 bushels Notification of this approval will be given to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission under Part 40 Regulations. title: NYSE American - Initial Pricing Regulatory Halt for Symbol GORO
raw_text:
NYSE American - Initial Pricing Regulatory Halt for Symbol GORO
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES; Services: Trading
Pursuant to the merger, Gold Resource Corporation (ticker GORO) and Goldgroup Mining Inc. (ticker GGAZD) will be converted into a single publicly traded corporate entity. This new entity will assume the name " Goldgroup Mining Inc." and will trade under the ticker GORO with new CUSIP 38141A602 on July 20, 2026. Historical trade data will carry over from existing ticker GGAZD with existing CUSIP 38141A602. As a security that is the subject of an initial pricing on NYSE American and that has not been listed on a national securities exchange immediately prior to this initial listing, pursuant to NYSE American Rule 7.18E(e), the common shares issued by Goldgroup Mining Inc. will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE American has opened trading beginning on July 20, 2026.

title: DTN2026-13 - D-Wave Quantum Inc. to Begin Listing and Trading on Nasdaq on July 27, 2026
raw_text:
DTN2026-13 - D-Wave Quantum Inc. to Begin Listing and Trading on Nasdaq on July 27, 2026
News CategoryAlertMarketsWhat You Need To Know
Data Technical News#2026-013

On July 27, 2026, D-Wave Quantum Inc (NYSE: QBTS) is expected to be listed and begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the same ticker symbol QBTS.

Nasdaq requests that market data redistributors update their databases to reflect the new listing market for D-Wave Quantum Inc. after market close on July 24, 2026 and maintain historical data for QBTS prior to transfer date.

title: DTN2026-14 - Nasdaq Announces Retirement of Two Depth Solution Products
raw_text:
DTN2026-14 - Nasdaq Announces Retirement of Two Depth Solution Products
News CategoryAlertMarketsWhat You Need To Know
Data Technical News#2026-014The Nasdaq Stock Market

As a part of its 23/5 market expansion, Nasdaq is retiring Depth Data products, Nasdaq Level 2 and Nasdaq Totalview Aggregated. Pending SEC review.

Customers are encouraged to migrate to Nasdaq TotalView or TotalView Plus offerings during the parallel migration period lasting until November 20th, 2026.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Tradr 2X Short SNDK Daily ETF (SNDQ) (SNDQ1)
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Tradr 2X Short SNDK Daily ETF (SNDQ) (SNDQ1)
Listing Alert MIAX Options July 20, 2026 | 12:07 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Tradr 2X Short SNDK Daily ETF (SNDQ) (SNDQ1) Tradr 2X Short SNDK Daily ETF (SNDQ) has announced a reverse stock split effective for Tuesday, July 21, 2026. SNDQ1 will be available for trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Tuesday, July 21, 2026. SNDQ1 will not be available for trading on the MIAX Emerald Options Exchange. No options on the underlying SNDQ will be available for trading on Tuesday, July 21, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in SNDQ will be canceled at the close of business on Monday, July 20, 2026. SNDQ will resume trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Wednesday, July 22, 2026. Please Note: SNDQ1 options will trade on Cloud 12 on the MIAX Options Exchange, on Cloud 8 on the MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and on Cloud 5 on the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange. Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. SNDQ should be used for class assignments in SNDQ1 via MFP All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. All changes made after 6:00 PM ET on a given day will be effective two trading days later. MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Primary Lead Market Maker (PLMM) assignments and un-assignments will not be supported via the MFP. Additional details about the Tradr 2X Short SNDK Daily ETF (SNDQ) reverse stock split are provided in the attached OCC Memo. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308. SNDQ Reverse Split 59359

title: MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Planned Internal Network Switch Refresh, Maintenance and Optional Testing
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Planned Internal Network Switch Refresh, Maintenance and Optional Testing
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald July 20, 2026 | 02:57 PM MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Planned Internal Network Switch Refresh, Maintenance and Optional Testing MIAX Emerald Options Exchange will refresh switches and perform internal maintenance on the following dates: Phase 1 - Friday, August 7, 2026, post close trading Phase 2 - Friday, August 14, 2026, post close trading Following completion of the maintenance, firms can perform the following optional verifications on Saturday, August 8, 2026 and Saturday, August 15, 2026: Verify connectivity to the subscribed unicast services (i.e. establish application-level socket connections to MEO, MEI, FOI, CTD, etc., for all ports, across all CCs) Confirm heartbeat receipt for both the A & B market data feeds for all subscribed multicast feeds across each CC Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Planned Network Switch Hardware Refresh for the 10G ULL and 1G LL Extranet and Mandatory Testing
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Planned Network Switch Hardware Refresh for the 10G ULL and 1G LL Extranet and Mandatory Testing
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Pearl Equities July 20, 2026 | 02:55 PM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Planned Network Switch Hardware Refresh for the 10G ULL and 1G LL Extranet and Mandatory Testing On Friday, August 21, 2026, MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange will refresh customer facing 10G ULL and 1G LL extranet edge switches after the close of trading at 8:00 p.m. EDT. All MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange Extranet edge switches will be replaced one to one with an updated model and all firm Cross Connects (CCs) will be moved to the same ports on the new switches. There are no changes required by firms for this hardware upgrade. Following the upgrade, firms will be required to test all Pearl Equities 10G ULL and 1G LL CCs . Details on testing and verifications: Testing will be held on Saturday, August 22, 2026, from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. EDT. Firms are required to perform the following verifications: Verify light levels and IP routing. Verify all 10G ULL and 1G LL CCs by logging into each subscribed application for each CC (i.e. establish application-level socket connections to MEO, FOI, CTD, etc., for all ports, across all CCs) and verify the login. Confirm heartbeat receipt for both the A & B market data feeds for all subscribed multicast feeds across each CC. After verifying connectivity, firms must email MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com . Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: Electronic Delivery of Information Under the Federal Securities Laws
raw_text:
Electronic Delivery of Information Under the Federal Securities Laws

The Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC" or the "Commission") is proposing Regulation E-Delivery. The proposed rule sets forth conditions for covered entities to deliver covered information to covered recipients electronically without first obtaining their affirmative consent. The proposed rule further establishes conditions under which the Commission would consider delivery requirements under the Federal securities laws to be satisfied by electronic delivery. The Commission also is proposing to rescind the rule providing alternative means for registered investment companies to satisfy shareholder report transmission requirements, and to amend rules addressing the dissemination of proxy materials and tender offer materials.

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Roll Out Schedule for August 2026 Enhancements
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Trading Alert, Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Roll Out Schedule for August 2026 Enhancements
Trading Alert, Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Options July 21, 2026 | 02:34 PM MIAX Options Exchange - Roll Out Schedule for August 2026 Enhancements As previously announced in the March 30, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options Exchange will roll out the following functionality in August 2026: Available Tuesday, August 25, 2026 Quote Width Relief Notification Functionality This optional feature allows members to subscribe to the “Free Trading Quote Width Relief Notification” to receive a real-time notification when MIAX grants relief to the valid quote width parameters during trading hours. This relief is only applicable for the current trading day. This enhancement is contained within the MEI Interface (v2.11) release. This feature requires certification prior to being used in production. Mass Cancel Trigger Reason on Line Disconnect This optional feature allows members to receive a mass cancel trigger reason specific to Line Disconnect available in the “Simple Quote or Complex Liquidity Protection Trigger Notification”. This enhancement is contained within the MEI Interface (v2.11) release. This feature requires certification prior to being used in production. Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed Update This enhancement allows members to identify the Origin of the liquidity that initiated a Liquidity Seeking Event (LSE) via the new “Origin” field in both the Simple and Complex Liquidity Seeking Event Notifications. Note that a blank Origin will be sent in the event an LSE is initiated by multiple liquidities with different origin types. This enhancement is contained within the AIS Interface (v2.6) release. On the date of activation, the updated Simple and Complex Liquidity Seeking Event Notifications with the new Origin field will replace the current version of each of these messages. Available Monday, August 31, 2026 Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Functionality This optional feature enhances the Mass Cancellation functionality by providing members the ability to remove a subset of its quotes in the system atomically. With the introduction of a new field “SLAP Codes” in the Simple Bulk Quote Message, members can specify custom SLAP codes on some or all of its liquidity, providing more granular control of the Mass Liquidity Cancel Request when attempting to “Selectively Purge” specific targeted liquidity. This enhancement is contained within the MEI Interface (v2.11) release. This feature requires certification prior to being used in production. Aggregate Risk Manager (ARM) Origin Multiplier Functionality This optional feature allows members to configure an “ARM Origin Multiplier” for each contra-side origin type. The multiplier will be applied to each ARM-eligible trade size based on the contra-side origin type, allowing members to establish a custom ARM profile individually weighted by contra-side origin type. Members wishing to only apply an Origin Multiplier to select origin types should set the origin types they do not wish to change to a value of 10 (i.e., use the original trade size with multiplier 1x). Note that for firms using old application protocols, their current settings will extend to all contra-types at 100% weightings by default. This enhancement is contained within the MEI Interface (v2.11) release. This feature requires certification prior to being used in production. As the SLAP and ARM Origin Multiplier enhancements are contained within the same MEI Interface (v2.11) release, firms looking to use the SLAP functionality must additionally code to the new ARM Settings Update Request (Message Type “RS”) or vice versa. Postponed to a Future Release Market Maker on PRIME/cPRIME Contra Functionality This optional feature would allow a Market Maker (MM) origin on the contra side of PRIME and cPRIME via the “CustomerOrFirm” field in the enhanced New Order Cross and New Order Cross Multileg messages. This enhancement will be removed from the FOI Interface (v2.7) release. This feature activation will be announced at a future date. Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to discuss any specific issues, update configurations and to arrange interface testing.

title: SIAC Support of 2026 SIFMA Industry-Wide Business Continuity Testing
raw_text:
SIAC Support of 2026 SIFMA Industry-Wide Business Continuity Testing
July 21, 2025 To: OPRA Multicast Subscribers Subject: SIAC Support of 2026 SIFMA Industry-Wide Business Continuity Testing What You Should Know: As part of the annual SIFMA industry test, OPRA will support BCP/DR testing. https://www.sifma.org/resources/general/industry-wide-business-continuity-test/ Testing Dates/Times: OPRA will run out of the DR site on the following dates and times: • BCP/DR Pre-Test #1: Saturday, September 26, 2026 - 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM, ET • BCP/DR Pre-Test #2: Saturday, October 10, 2026 - 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM, ET • BCP/DR Test: Saturday, October 24, 2026 - 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM, ET Documentation OPRA specifications can be obtained at https://www.opraplan.com/document-library . Technical Questions • For Technical related questions, please generate an email to CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com. • Multicast Data Feed Subscribers can also contact the SIAC NMS Product Management Desk at 212-656-8177, Option 2, Mon-Fri 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, ET. If support team members are engaged with other customers, please leave a detailed voice message stating the purpose of your call, which will produce an email of your message to the support team. OPRA Notifications • To automatically receive these notifications and OPRA Email Alerts, you are required to subscribe at: https://www.opraplan.com . Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005
